title: Beginner
outcome: Start from zero, understand basic classroom English, and handle short real-life interactions with support.

chapters.title: Sound, Survival English, and First Sentences
chapters.summary: Decode English sounds, survive basic interactions, and build first sentence patterns.
contexts: asking for simple help in public
lexicon: the full set of English letters
the way a letter or word is pronounced
to say or write letters in order
the look of something like red or blue
the word people use to identify you
something you ask to get information
lessons: Letters, Sounds, and Your First English Words
recognize English letters and say short words clearly
identify basic objects and describe them
My Name, My City, My Country
point to things near and far
understand and respond to tiny commands
Everyday Actions: Eat, Go, Come, Do
yes-no questions with be and do
combine early beginner language into one short introduction

chapters.title: Home, Family, and Daily Routine
chapters.summary: Speak about home life, family relationships, and everyday routines in connected sentences.
contexts: describing your day to a friend
lexicon: a part of the house like a bedroom or kitchen
the place where food is cooked
the first meal of the day
the middle meal of the day
to make something neat and not dirty
lessons: describe home spaces and common objects
talk about repeated actions in order
Food, Water, Tea, and the Kitchen
ask for and talk about food items
say what you enjoy or avoid
talk about work done at home
My Day from Morning to Night

chapters.title: Places, Time, and Moving Around
chapters.summary: Handle location, time, transport, weather, and travel basics for independent movement.
lexicon: a place where people buy things
a place where trains or buses arrive and leave
the pass you need for travel
a piece of clothing for the upper body
a road vehicle for many passengers
the amount of money you pay
lessons: name common places around the city
Turn Left, Go Straight, Come Back
By Bus, Auto, Metro, or Cab
transport vocabulary and by plus transport
Buying Tickets and Asking the Price
present simple for facts and choices
hold a short location conversation end to end
review of directions, time, and transport

chapters.title: People, Feelings, and Social Basics
chapters.summary: Describe people, talk about feelings, and build social comfort in everyday conversations.
lexicon: kind and easy to talk to
to ask someone to join you
to speak to someone by phone
very happy and full of energy
a person who lives near you
lessons: talk about appearance and personality simply
would you like to and let's
ask for size, color, and price
keep a short social conversation going

chapters.title: First Real-World Conversations
chapters.summary: Apply beginner English to school, work, health, shopping, and future plans in practical dialogs.
contexts: talking about what happened and what is next
lexicon: the place where many people work
the list of food or drink choices
the place where money services happen
money returned after payment or a difference
what you decide to do later
a request for food or goods
lessons: introduce yourself in a simple work setting
order food and respond to simple questions
At a Bank or Service Desk
ask for help in a formal public place
buy things, ask prices, and confirm quantities
countable nouns and how much/how many
What Are You Going to Do Next?
connect beginner grammar patterns without freezing
carry a longer guided conversation independently
